title: Mildly Briny Roman Chicken Pasta
description: A date-night Italian dish featuring dried apricots and a soffritto base, this recipe is sugar-free friendly and offers a unique blend of briny and bouncy textures.
instructions: Heat the olive oil in a large skillet over medium heat.
Add the chopped onion, carrots, and celery and cook until the vegetables are softened.
Add the minced garlic and cook for 1 minute.
Add the chicken to the skillet and cook until browned on all sides and cooked through.
Add the chopped dried apricots, anchovy fillets, salt, and black pepper to the skillet and stir to combine.
Cook the spaghetti in a large pot of boiling salted water until al dente.
Reserve 1 cup of pasta water and then drain the spaghetti.
Add the cooked spaghetti to the skillet with the chicken and vegetable mixture.
Toss the spaghetti in the skillet, adding some reserved pasta water if the mixture seems dry.
Stir in the grated parmesan cheese until melted and well combined.
Serve the pasta hot, garnished with additional parmesan cheese if desired.
